inputs.fcf_source: Normalized 2026 owner cash: YTD royalty payments C$2.70M (Jan 1-Jun 1 NR) plus Uis run-rate US$4-4.5M/year (corporate presentation Feb 2026) and HVC YTD C$1.19M; divided by ~55.1M pro-forma shares post-Uis issuance
inputs.normalization_note: Royalty holding company; owner cash = cash royalty receipts net of minimal G&A. FY2025 MD&A on SEDAR+ not yet harvested; base uses H1 2026 disclosed payments and operator-guided ramp, not GAAP net income.

bear.notes: Copper/tin prices weaken; HVC mine life uncertainty; Copper Mountain carveout delays; junior multiple compression

base.notes: McIlvenna Bay Q3 2026 start; Uis steady state; Copper Mountain payments after US$10M carveout; HVC MLE to 2046

bull.notes: Four cash-flowing royalties by 2027; Sal de Los Angeles construction; Sunnyside close; copper/tin tailwinds; re-rate toward 1.0x NAV peer multiple

optionality_gate.notes: Corporate presentation Feb 2026 shows EVR at ~0.75x P/NAV vs junior royalty peers at 1.0-1.7x; at C$2.80 vs ~C$4.26 implied NAV (~34% discount). Lawrence owner-cash path is stance gate; development royalties sized in overlay only.

nav_overlay.nav_source: EVR/presentations-and-media/corporate-presentation-2026-02.pdf — peer P/NAV table (~0.75x at C$3.21; C$163M market cap / 50.9M shares)

option_scan.question: GAAP book misstates core assets?
option_scan.evidence: Royalty interests carried at cost; economic value tied to commodity prices and reserve growth, not marked to market (CSE listing statement / FY2025 FS per SEDAR+ reference in June 1 NR)

option_scan.question: Undeveloped reserves / dormant royalty?
option_scan.evidence: 14-asset portfolio: 7 exploration royalties (Hanson Lake, Bigstone, Pine Bay, etc.); Sal de Los Angeles lithium permitted but pre-production; Copper Mountain US$3.3M carveout remaining

option_scan.question: In-business loss segment?
option_scan.evidence: Royalty-only model; no operating loss segments

option_scan.question: Backlog / contracted revenue not in FCF path?
option_scan.evidence: McIlvenna Bay C$0.75/t royalty expected Q3 2026; Copper Mountain 5% NSR after US$10M pre-tax exclusion (US$6.7M paid to date per June 1 NR)

option_scan.question: Private or illiquid stakes below fair value?
option_scan.evidence: Cash and publicly tradeable securities plus convertible debenture in private entity (~C$9M per presentation pro-forma cap table)

option_scan.question: Transitory distribution / legal recovery?
option_scan.evidence: No dividend program yet; reinvestment/acquisition phase

option_scan.question: Embedded product option already in revenue?
option_scan.evidence: HVC 0.51% NPI and Uis tin GRR already in YTD C$2.70M payments

nav_overlay_options.label: Copper Mountain 5% NSR (post US$10M exclusion)
nav_overlay_options.base_terminal_rationale: US$3.3M carveout remaining; Hudbay 2027-2028 copper guidance +92% vs 2026; not in Lawrence FCF base until payments commence

nav_overlay_options.label: McIlvenna Bay C$0.75/t net tonnage royalty
nav_overlay_options.base_terminal_rationale: Eldorado commercial production expected Q3 2026; partial 2026 contribution in base growth, full ramp in overlay

nav_overlay_options.label: Litio Angeles Argentina 2% NSR lithium
nav_overlay_options.base_terminal_rationale: Permitted 10 ktpa LCE Phase 1; construction timing tied to lithium market and RIGI application; zero in Lawrence base

nav_overlay_options.label: Exploration-stage royalties (7 assets)
nav_overlay_options.base_terminal_rationale: Pre-resource or early-stage; no disclosed cash flows; explicit zero after option scan

growth_explanation.base_mechanism: Ramp from one to four cash-flowing royalties in 2026 (HVC, Uis, McIlvenna Bay, Copper Mountain) per June 1 operator updates
growth_explanation.bear_falsifier: Sustained copper/tin price decline or Teck HVC mine-life extension delay
growth_explanation.bull_falsifier: Faster Copper Mountain carveout clearance plus Sal de Los Angeles construction start with peer multiple re-rate above 1.0x NAV

catalyst_paths.impact: C$0.75/t royalty on Eldorado copper-zinc output
Extends 0.51% NPI on Canada's largest copper mine
Commodity downturn; junior royalty multiple compression; acquisition overpay

paths.label: Growth theory (pre-falsifier)
paths.weight_why: Segment blend before falsifier pass; diagnostic only when it differs from falsifier-adjusted.
weight_falsifiers: Included at full weight alongside falsifier-adjusted without divergence note

paths.label: Bear scenario (Lawrence)
paths.weight_why: Downside stress; low weight because bear is a sensitivity, not a competing explanation of base mechanics.
weight_falsifiers: Bear assumptions become base case in filings (e.g. sustained volume collapse)
paths.hard_to_vary: yes: asymmetric low weight is structural

paths.label: Bull scenario (Lawrence)
paths.weight_why: Upside stress probe; higher than bear because optionality archetype has skew, but still below primary paths.
weight_falsifiers: Bull growth sustained in filings for 4+ quarters without falsifier trigger

qualitative_adjustments.factor: No dated predictive attribute at spot
qualitative_adjustments.rationale: Scarcity is descriptive; no timed mispricing catalyst in base
qualitative_adjustments.falsifier: Cross-check names dated catalyst with timeline

deutsch_checks.reach: Weights encode evidence tier (A primary → D external), not fitted to hit a target IRR

synthesis.notes: Capstone IRR combining filings, segments, overlays, third-party, qualitative

context_overlay.disclaimer: Context only. Tailwinds inform stance and overlay sizing; they do not auto-inflate Lawrence base IRR. Promotion to base case requires [HUMAN REVIEW].
